research_series.series_name: Blood Pressure and Orthostatic Physiology Variation Tree:Volume Regulation,Dizziness,Brain Fog,Fatigue,and Spaceflight Deconditioning

core_branches.chain: orthostatic_HR_increase + dizziness + no_BP_drop -> POTS-like orthostatic intolerance branch
required_sources: active stand or tilt table HR/BP
active stand or tilt table BP

core_branches.chain: postflight_or_bed_rest + reduced_plasma_volume + orthostatic_intolerance -> spaceflight deconditioning volume branch

guardrails: Do not infer POTS from tachycardia outside orthostatic testing.
Do not infer hypovolemia from urine concentration alone.
Do not infer cerebral hypoperfusion from dizziness alone.
Do not infer dehydration from low BP alone.
Do not compare orthostatic tests without protocol duration/posture/medication context.
Plasma volume loss is important but not sufficient for postural intolerance in all individuals.
research_gaps: Few studies link urine osmolality/specific gravity directly to hypovolemia diagnosis in orthostatic settings.
Limited data on cognitive outcomes linked to measured cerebral perfusion during orthostatic stress.
Sparse high-quality long-term salt/fluid intervention trials.
Lack of standardized active stand vs tilt table protocols across populations.
